title: France: Montreuil, Baugé Opera, Auvergne & Millau

main_route.original_wording: Martello Tower No 24 at near Hythe, Dymchurch
main_route.excerpt: We wanted to see a restored Martello Tower No 24 at near Hythe, Dymchurch, but when we got there, it was closed

main_route.inference_reason: Diary says they caught a 'train' and reached the French campsite in under 1.5 hours, immediately after the Hythe/Dymchurch stop; this indicates the Eurotunnel shuttle (Folkestone-Coquelles), not a sea ferry.
main_route.excerpt: We caught an earlier [?train] + in under 1½ hours we reached the campsite
La Calotterie near Montreuil. Very green, a grassy valley, cold showers, fresh bread deliveries for breakfast.
So we drove to Abbeville. After a 2 hour wait, the garage reopened ... the Abbeville garage used one that someone else had already ordered & we were mended.
we back-tracked about 12 miles to Sacé, a drive-in campsite in a village, pretty beech hedges, choose your own plot
We drove on to Baugé, where Venetia Grommett's mother puts on operas ... At 4.40 we set off to walk to the Opera - Richard the Lionheart, Handel.

main_route.original_wording: La Vielle Morte, Ids St. Rozh
main_route.excerpt: he found a local campsite with a swimming pool. It's called "La Vielle Morte"(!) Ids St. Rozh ... a very eccentric campsite in scorched orchards.
We set off & shopped outside St. Flour.
We drove on to the Viaduc de Garabit, a railway bridge built by Eiffel to the design of Léon Boyer. 1882-4 ... It's very impressive.
We drove on to [?Camping at the Farm] ... Fridefont. It's a magnificent valley ... they have put up the tent ... We had a mega meal at the farmhouse
We then drove on to see the Millan Bridge. It only opened in April ... We climbed up to the viewing platform. It's very beautiful, elegant.
Camp St. Martin ... We could just see it from the camp site ... Swimming pool, v. clean., washing machine
We drove on to Entraygues after lunch ... The Entraygues campsite is called Val dos Saures (Valley of the lizards?) pitch 104 one of the best campsites we've ever been in
We packed up + wandered along hair-pin bends at 20 mph to Conques. The Abbey is as lovely as ever. After pizza in the square, we looked at the Treasury
We wandered further, with more hair-pin bends to Cassianousc ... The Hotel Beau Sejour is booked for both evenings that we're here, so we reserved for tomorrow's lunch.
We set off for Figiac along beautiful small roads + reached it before lunch ... In Figiac we saw the church with a beautiful, painted crypt-chapel.
We drove on to Lissac et Mouret, M. GAY Gerard, a lovely campsite in an enormous field with walnut trees. The farmhouse has a pool & trampolines & a bar.
We drove off through lovely country side & reached Saillant, le Bontet the campsite is next to a river ... We walked down to look at the chapel, which has windows by Marc Chagall
We drove to Uzerche, a town that in about 850 endured a 7 year siege against the Saracens ... We had delicious chips for lunch.
On to David's at his new house. All the family are there. We walked through beautiful woods & went to supper at Margaret's hotel. at Chamberet.
Simon, Eve, Charles, Margaret & Helen left after a leisurely breakfast. We look at the view from Mont Gargan. We drove north
we had already put up the tent at Courcheveigny municipal camping ... near Chambord ... We went back to Courcheveigny for supper.
We picked up M+H & drove past Chateau de Chambord.
Just south of Amiens, St. Fuscien, is Margaret's hotel. We dropped off her case ... we found a very good place to eat supper, overlooking the boat-gardens, Hortillages.
We looked at Amiens Cathedral & set off for the Channel.

main_route.original_wording: the Channel ... get on the train
main_route.inference_reason: Return crossing: diary describes a gas check and barrier queue 'to get on the train' at the Channel, i.e. the Eurotunnel shuttle from the French side (Coquelles).
main_route.excerpt: we could hurry through to get on the train (the numbering system for calling us was unclear) but then we just sat infront of the barrier + missed the train.
We got to Di Simmonds in Oldham at 3.30. There we showed her grandson, little Harry, the camping van.
Then we took Margaret + Helen back to Trumpley + then home to 86.

crossings.excerpt: We caught an earlier [?train] + in under 1½ hours we reached the campsite
we could hurry through to get on the train ... but then we just sat infront of the barrier + missed the train.

stops.excerpt: Simon + Eve walked from Cassianousc to [?Catre] Calvinet in the afternoon, over the fields + a slight ridge. Lots of grasshoppers.
Mid morning we left for La[?tte] des Insects at Nede. It was fascinating, with a beautiful observation ants' nest, a tunnel with lit-up ant passage ways underneath.
We drove on to Lake Vassivière, where people swam. Sally & Maia joined us & we had tea before driving home to Bas Noir.
Simon & Eve drove to Treignac & bought a b.b.q meat. We swam.

unresolved.issue: Place where Margaret & Helen were dropped on the way home (UK); spelling uncertain and cannot be confidently located. Likely a village/house name. Brief drop-off, not an overnight stop.
unresolved.excerpt: Then we took Margaret + Helen back to Trumpley + then home to 86.

boundary_flags.note: B_0097_L opens mid-page with the tail of the previous (non-trip) entry (Margaret, the pond, Skye/Thorpeness pictures) before the July 23rd heading where this trip begins. Trip content starts at the 'July 23rd Sunday' heading.

summary: In late July to mid-August 2006 Simon, Eve and son Charles took the camping van through the Channel Tunnel and down through France: a first night near Montreuil (with a van repair detour via Abbeville), then Sacé and Baugé for a Handel opera, on through the Auvergne (Ids-Saint-Roch, the Viaduc de Garabit and a farm campsite at Fridefont) to the new Viaduc de Millau. They worked west through Entraygues, Conques, Cassaniouze, Figeac and Lissac-et-Mouret (visiting friend John Hurman), then north via Le Saillant and Uzerche to stay with David's family at Chamberet, before heading home via Cour-Cheverny, Chambord and Amiens.
extraction_notes: Sketch page B_0101_R is a campsite-layout/pitch drawing of the Fridefont farm site (note 'hoops adapter? to camp electric cables'), NOT a route map, so evidence-hierarchy rule 1 (route sketches) does not apply; route order is taken from the dated travel narrative and physical page order. Crossings: the diary repeatedly says 'train' for both Channel crossings, indicating Eurotunnel (Folkestone-Coquelles) rather than a ferry; marked inferred=true. The Hythe/Dymchurch Martello Tower stop and 'under 1.5 hours to the campsite' support a Kent/Eurotunnel routing. Date anomalies in source: B_0106_R is transcribed as 2012 and B_0109_L is headed 'Sunday August 4th' (Aug 4 2006 was a Friday); both are clearly part of this 2006 trip and are ordered by physical page sequence and narrative flow. 'Bas Noir' is David's house at/near Chamberet, treated as the same overnight base. Excursions: Calvinet walk from the Cassaniouze base; Nedde insect museum, Lac de Vassivière and Treignac as day trips returning to Chamberet (Bas Noir). 'Trumpley' (UK drop-off near home) could not be confidently located and is queued as unresolved. Diary page numbering inside the bundle ('no') runs ~35-50, used for diary_page_range.
